history.content:
<thought>
I’m at row 02 column B facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 04 column D. From here, moving forward would go into lava at row 02 column C (`L`), which is unsafe. I need to move away from lava by going down: I will turn right once so that I face down (`v`), then I can proceed downward toward the open cells.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 02 column B facing down (`v`). The goal is at row 04 column D. The cell to the right (row 02 column C) is lava `L`, so I shouldn’t go that way. Instead, I can move forward to row 03 column B (which is `.`) to progress toward the open corridor leading to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 03 column B facing down (`v`). The goal is at row 04 column D. From my position, going forward would hit lava at row 04 column B (`L`), so I should turn to avoid it. The cell to the right (row 03 column C) is floor (`.`), which is a safer direction to progress toward the goal column D, so I will turn right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 03 column B, facing left (`<`). The goal is at row 04 column D. From my current position, moving forward would hit a wall at row 03 column A (not safe). So I should turn to re-orient toward open space: turning right from `<` to face up (`^`) will allow movement without immediately running into lava/walls.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 03 column B, facing up (^). The goal is at row 04 column D. Ahead of me (row 02 column B) is empty, but that doesn’t make progress toward the goal. I should turn right to face east and head toward column D while avoiding lava at row 02 column C and row 04 column C.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 03 column B facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 04 column D. The cell in front of me (row 03 column C) is `.` and is safe, and moving right will bring me closer to the goal through the open corridor.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 03 column C, facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 04 column D. The cell directly forward (row 03 column D) is floor (`.`), but from there the goal would be blocked by a wall at row 04 column E and not directly reachable. Better approach: turn right to face down and move toward row 04 column C, which is floor (`.`), then proceed to the goal at row 04 column D.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 03 column C facing down (`v`). The goal is at row 04 column D. From my current position, I should face right to move into row 03 column D, then proceed to the goal. So first, I need to turn right (clockwise) from down to left? Actually down (`v`) turning right(clockwise) faces left (`<`). Turning left(counter-clockwise) from down faces right (`>`). I will TURN_LEFT to face right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 03 column C facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 04 column D. To reach it, I should move forward to row 03 column D first, then move down into the goal. From my current position, the cell in front (row 03 column D) is safe (`.`), so I will move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 03 column D, facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 04 column D. The cell directly in front (row 03 column E) is a wall `#`, so I can’t move forward. I need to move down, so I will turn right to face down.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 03 column D facing down (`v`). The goal is at row 04 column D. The cell directly in front (row 04 column D) contains `G`, and it’s safe to enter. So I should move forward into the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
